signatureQuote: I've learned that patience and curiosity get you further than rushing through life ever did.
imageStyle: Casual-professional: approachable, cardigan or comfortable blouse, reading glasses on chain
privacyPreference: Moderate - comfortable with general info, cautious about financial details and exact address
politicalOrientation: Centre-left, moderate Labour supporter - values NHS and public services
communicationStyle: Clear, straightforward explanations with narrative context. Prefers understanding the 'why' behind things, not just bullet points
tonePreference: Friendly but respectful - dislikes overly casual or patronizing tones
languageRegister: Moderately formal - consistent use of 'please' and 'thank you'
channelPreferences: Phone for important matters, email for non-urgent, WhatsApp for family. Avoids social media
energyFluctuations: Strong mornings, dip 2-3 PM after lunch, moderate early evening, tired by 8 PM
primaryHobbies: Gardening (vegetables and flowers), reading historical fiction and biographies, baking for family
secondaryInterests: Nature documentaries, crossword puzzles, community centre events, library volunteering weekly
mediaConsumption: BBC TV (news, dramas), Radio 4, physical newspaper, occasional YouTube gardening videos
activityPreference: Mix of solo (reading, gardening) and small group (book club, coffee). Prefers in-person to online
lifestyleOrientation: Traditional but adaptable - values routine and familiar comforts, willing to learn when beneficial
hobbyTime: 10-15 hours per week, mostly mornings and weekends
academicPerformance: B/C average - solid student but not academic
fieldOfStudy: Secretarial studies: typing, shorthand, business administration (1970s)
previousJobs: School secretary (20 years), GP surgery administrator (15 years), manufacturing firm secretary (8 years), temp secretarial roles (early career)
roleAuthority: Mid-level administrative - autonomy in role, reported to head teachers/practice managers
incomeLevel: £16,000-18,000 per year (state pension plus modest occupational pension) - comfortable but careful
homeEnvironment: Quiet residential street, comfortable dated décor, good natural light, small back garden, reliable broadband, spare bedroom for grandchildren visits
financialHabits: Definite saver - 'waste not, want not' upbringing. Budgets carefully, shops sales, avoids debt. Keeps written household budget
longTermGoals: Maintain financial independence, leave inheritance for children, ensure funds for potential care needs, keep budget for treats with grandchildren
nextPurchase: Replace old washing machine, book coach trip to Lake District with friend Joan next spring
primaryTransport: Bus (free bus pass) and walking. Son drives for larger shopping/medical appointments
mobilityFrequency: Daily short walks (15-20 min), bus 2-3x/week to town, weekly family car visits
mobilityPurpose: Local errands, town centre visits, library, visiting friends, family visits
accessibilityConsiderations: Generally mobile but avoids uneven pavements, prefers routes with benches. Stopped driving 3 years ago (reaction time, parking stress)
mobilityRange: Local: 2-mile radius on foot, within town by bus. Occasional day trips with friends/family
healthStatus: Generally good for age, mobile and independent
chronicConditions: Controlled high blood pressure (daily medication), mild osteoarthritis in knees/hands (manageable with paracetamol), slight hearing loss
disabilityStatus: Mild sensory impairments - hearing and vision
accessibilityNeeds: Reading glasses (progressive lenses), hearing aids in social situations, prefers larger text, avoids stairs when possible (prefers lifts/ground floor)
shortTermPriorities: Stay healthy and independent, quality time with grandchildren, digitize family photos (grandson helping), maintain garden, keep home well-maintained
longTermGoalsPriorities: Remain in own home as long as possible, see grandchildren grow up, leave affairs in order, continue community volunteering, write down family stories and recipes
deviceOwnership: Basic Samsung Android smartphone, iPad tablet (gift from children 3 years ago)
primaryDevice: Tablet for news/emails/video calls. Phone for calls/messages
operatingSystem: Mixed - Android phone, iOS tablet (finds this confusing)
digitalLiteracy: Low to moderate - can do basic tasks (email, WhatsApp, video calls, online banking with help). Struggles with updates, troubleshooting, anything beyond routine. Often calls grandson for help
securityPractices: Cautious but not sophisticated. Uses same few passwords (knows she shouldn't). Wary of scams, won't click unexpected links. Doesn't understand VPNs, encryption, detailed privacy settings. Covers PIN at cashpoints
trustPropensity: Moderate to low - naturally skeptical of new technology. Trusts established institutions (bank, NHS, government sites), suspicious of new apps/unfamiliar online retailers
digitalActivityFrequency: Limited range: email (daily), WhatsApp (few times/week), video calls (weekly), online banking (monthly with trepidation), BBC news, occasional YouTube. That's it.
techAttitude: Low to moderate ATI - sees technology as useful but often frustrating. Uses it to stay connected with family but doesn't enjoy learning new systems. Misses simpler days
professionalSkills: Excellent organizational/administrative skills, fast accurate typing (touch typing trained), good written communication and attention to detail, patient communication especially with parents/children/patients, basic bookkeeping, knowledge of NHS/educational administration (outdated)
softSkills: Strong interpersonal communication, practical problem-solving, patience, active listening, collaboration in small teams, mentoring junior staff, empathy particularly with worried parents/patients
learningAgility: Moderate but declining - willing to learn if clear benefit (learned tablet for grandchildren video calls), but finds new technology frustrating and slow
skillMotivation: Needs repeated practice and clear patient instruction. Much less confident trying new things than at 50
certifications: RSA Typing and Secretarial certificates (1973 - outdated), expired First Aid certificate, no recent certifications
extroversion: Ambivert - balanced approach to social interaction. Comfortable in both social situations and solitary activities. Can enjoy group settings but also appreciates quiet time alone. Adaptable energy levels depending on context. Moderate-sized social circle, selective about engagements. Works well in teams and independently.
agreeableness: More competitive and skeptical than cooperative. Values directness and honesty over social niceties. Not afraid to disagree or challenge when believes she's right. Prioritizes own interests and goals. Less concerned with maintaining harmony if it means compromising on important matters. Perceived as tough-minded or blunt. Comfortable with debate, not easily manipulated by emotional appeals. Respects strength and competence over warmth.
conscientiousness: Organized, disciplined, and goal-oriented. Reliable and responsible, follows through on commitments consistently. Prefers structure and planning over spontaneity. Detail-oriented and thorough. Takes pride in doing things well. Well-prepared for situations, keeps environment organized. Strong self-discipline, persists through challenges. Values efficiency, order, and competence.
neuroticism: Generally calm and emotionally resilient. Handles stress well, doesn't get overwhelmed easily. Typically in stable, positive mood without frequent anxiety or worry. Stays composed under pressure, bounces back from disappointments quickly. Not easily rattled by criticism or setbacks. Can experience negative emotions but they don't dominate or significantly disrupt functioning.
openness: Intellectually curious with appreciation for novelty. Drawn to new ideas, experiences, and ways of thinking. Enjoys abstract discussions and thinks creatively about problems. Appreciates art, culture, and intellectual pursuits. Imaginative, thinks beyond conventional boundaries. Embraces change and variety, finds routine somewhat boring. Willing to question traditions and explore unconventional perspectives. Intellectually engaged, constantly seeking to expand understanding.
